page: Amazon Web Services c7gn.metal is a bare-metal, compute-optimized server powered by 64 dedicated physical cores of the AWS Graviton ARMv8 Neoverse-V1 processor architecture. Operating at a clock speed of 2.6 GHz, this arm64 platform features 128.0 GB of DDR5 memory, providing a resource density of 2.0 GB per core. The server does not include local storage or integrated GPUs, relying instead on network-attached storage and its baseline network bandwidth of 200 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates strong multi-core processing and memory subsystem performance. The server achieves top-tier results in Geekbench multi-core execution, software compilation with Clang, and Passmark memory and database operations. Its memory bandwidth for large-block read operations is rated in the top 25 percent, reaching 277.09 GB/sec. Additionally, the platform demonstrates strong performance in static web serving throughput and Redis SET operations, though single-threaded tasks and small-model text generation show average to poor relative performance.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the c7gn.metal offers high resource density and high-bandwidth networking without the overhead of virtualization. This makes it highly suitable for network-intensive and compute-heavy workloads. Recommended applications include software compilation, database operations, high-throughput web serving, and multi-threaded parallel processing tasks that benefit from dedicated physical cores and high network throughput.
description: Amazon Web Services c7gn.metal is a bare-metal, compute-optimized server featuring 64 dedicated physical cores powered by the 2.6 GHz AWS Graviton ARMv8 Neoverse-V1 processor. Built on the arm64 architecture, it is equipped with 128.0 GB of DDR5 memory, yielding a 2.0 GB per core ratio, and delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 200 Gbps. The server lacks local storage and GPUs. In benchmark evaluations, the c7gn.metal demonstrates top-tier performance in multi-core software compilation, database operations, and memory bandwidth, alongside strong static web serving throughput. However, single-threaded performance and small-model text generation remain average to poor. This hardware profile is highly suited for network-intensive applications, multi-threaded parallel computing, software compilation, and database workloads requiring dedicated physical cores and high-speed network interfaces.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c7gn.metal is a bare-metal, compute-optimized server with 64 dedicated ARMv8 cores, 128 GB DDR5 RAM, and 200 Gbps network bandwidth, optimized for high-throughput database and compilation workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c7gn.metal is a bare-metal server with 64 Graviton cores, 128 GB DDR5 RAM, and 200 Gbps network bandwidth for compute-heavy workloads.
tagline: A bare-metal, compute-optimized ARM platform featuring sixty-four dedicated cores and high-speed networking for demanding multi-threaded and database workloads.
bullet_points: 64 dedicated physical AWS Graviton ARMv8 Neoverse-V1 cores at 2.6 GHz
128.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory with 2.0 GB per core density
200 Gbps baseline network bandwidth for high-throughput networking
Top-tier benchmark performance in software compilation and database operations
No local storage or GPU accelerators included
Best suited for network-intensive, multi-threaded, and compilation workloads
